rationale: Postcondition is genuinely unprovable from the spec. The ensures clause is (spec_for_r1 && spec_for_r2) ==> (r1.0 == r2.0 && r1.1 == r2.1). When the forall holds (so r1.0=r2.0=true), the !r1.0/!r2.0 conjuncts are vacuous and r1.1, r2.1 are unconstrained -- e.g. r1=(true,true) and r2=(true,false) both satisfy the spec but violate det_values_agree_equal. Tried empty proof, case split on r1.0 with direct asserts, and a global implication assert; all failed exactly on this branch. No proof statements can close this without strengthening values_agree's spec to constrain ret.1 when ret.0 is true.
